metadata.title: 英语语法体系
metadata.description: 完整的现代英语语法体系，涵盖词性、时态、句子分析三大模块
metadata.descriptionEn: Complete modern English grammar system covering parts of speech, tenses, and sentence analysis
metadata.source: Based on Oxford English Grammar, Cambridge Grammar, English Grammar in Use, and other authoritative sources

modules.name: 词性
modules.description: 英语词性分类体系，包含10大词性类别和82个子类型
modules.descriptionEn: English parts of speech classification system with 10 categories and 82 subtypes

modules.name: 时态
modules.description: 英语动词时态体系，包含12种基本时态、过去将来时、被动语态等
modules.descriptionEn: English verb tense system including 12 basic tenses, future in the past, passive voice

modules.name: 句子精解
modules.description: 英语句子分析体系，包含句子成分、句型、从句和特殊句式
modules.descriptionEn: English sentence analysis system covering sentence elements, types, clauses, and special patterns

overview.definition: 词性（又称词类）是根据词语在句子中的功能对其进行分类的类别。它们是英语语法的基本构建模块。现代语法认可10大词性类别。
overview.definitionEn: Parts of speech (word classes) are categories into which words are classified based on their functions in a sentence. They are the fundamental building blocks of English grammar. Modern grammar recognizes 10 parts of speech.
overview.classificationApproach: 现代语法分类（将限定词和冠词作为独立词性）
overview.classificationApproachEn: Modern grammar classification (treats determiners and articles as separate word classes)
keyPoints: 英语词性分为开放类（名词、动词、形容词、副词）和封闭类（代词、限定词、介词、连词、感叹词、冠词）
现代语法将限定词视为与形容词独立的词类
每个词性都有多种子类型，总计82个子类型
keyPointsEn: English parts of speech are divided into open classes (nouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs) and closed classes (pronouns, determiners, prepositions, conjunctions, interjections, articles)
Modern grammar treats determiners as a separate word class distinct from adjectives
Each part of speech has multiple subtypes, totaling 82 subtypes

categories.name: 名词
categories.definition: 名词是用来命名人、动物、地点、事物、品质、想法、活动或感情的词。名词在句子中可以充当主语、直接宾语、间接宾语、主语补足语、宾语补足语、同位语或修饰语。
categories.definitionEn: A noun is a word that names a person, animal, place, thing, quality, idea, activity, or feeling. A noun can function as the subject, direct object, indirect object, subject complement, object complement, appositive, or modifier in a sentence.
categories.function: 充当句子中的主语、宾语、补语、同位语等
categories.functionEn: Functions as subject, object, complement, appositive in sentences

subTypes.name: 普通名词
subTypes.definition: 人、地点或事物的一般名称（除非在句首，否则不大写）
subTypes.definitionEn: General names for people, places, or things (not capitalized unless beginning a sentence)

examples.sentence: The boy is playing.
She lives in a big city.
I am reading a book.
The teacher gave the students some books.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 可作为主语、宾语、补语；可以有单数或复数形式
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Acts as subject, object, complement; can be singular or plural

subTypes.name: 专有名词
subTypes.definition: 特定的人、地点或事物的名称；首字母始终大写
subTypes.definitionEn: Names of specific people, places, or things; always capitalized

examples.sentence: John is my friend.
Mary visited Paris last December.
I will see you on Monday.
They climbed Mount Everest.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 指代独特实体；通常不使用限定词
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Refers to unique entities; typically takes no determiner

subTypes.name: 具体名词
subTypes.definition: 可以通过五种感官感知到的名词（看得见、摸得着、听得见、闻得到、尝得出）
subTypes.definitionEn: Nouns that can be perceived by the five senses (seen, touched, heard, smelled, tasted)

examples.sentence: She picked up a beautiful flower from the garden.
I love the sound of rain.
The tree is very tall.
A butterfly landed on the flower.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 指代物理实体；可以是可数或不可数
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Refers to physical entities; can be countable or uncountable

subTypes.name: 抽象名词
subTypes.definition: 指无法通过物理感知的想法、概念、品质、情感或状态的名词
subTypes.definitionEn: Nouns that refer to ideas, concepts, qualities, emotions, or states that cannot be physically perceived

examples.sentence: His kindness and wisdom inspired everyone around him.
Wisdom comes with experience.
Love is a powerful emotion.
He showed great courage.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 通常不可数；表达无形概念
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Often uncountable; expresses intangible concepts

subTypes.name: 可数名词
subTypes.definition: 可以被计数的名词；有单数和复数形式；可以与数字连用
subTypes.definitionEn: Nouns that can be counted; have singular and plural forms; can be used with numbers

examples.sentence: I bought three apples and two oranges.
She has many books.
There are twenty students in the class.
They built a new house.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 使用复数标记(-s/-es)；与many, few, a few, several连用
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Takes plural marker (-s/-es); used with 'many', 'few', 'a few', 'several'

subTypes.name: 不可数名词/物质名词
subTypes.definition: 不能被单独计数的名词，又称物质名词；永远是单数形式
subTypes.definitionEn: Nouns that cannot be counted individually; also called Mass Nouns; always singular

examples.sentence: Please give me some water and advice.
I need some milk.
This information is useful.
We bought some new furniture.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 与much, little, a little, some连用；无复数形式；无定冠词
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Used with 'much', 'little', 'a little', 'some'; no plural form; no indefinite article

subTypes.name: 集合名词
subTypes.definition: 将一群人、动物或事物作为一个整体来命名的名词
subTypes.definitionEn: Nouns that name a group of people, animals, or things as a single unit

examples.sentence: The team is playing well.
My family is very supportive.
The flock of birds flew south.
The audience applauded loudly.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 可用单数或复数动词（美式英语通常用单数）
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Can take singular or plural verb (American English usually singular)

subTypes.name: 复合名词
subTypes.definition: 由两个或多个词组合在一起构成的名词（闭合式、连字符式或开放式）
subTypes.definitionEn: Nouns made up of two or more words joined together (closed, hyphenated, or open)

formationTypes.examples: bus stop, credit card, swimming pool, high school, post office

examples.sentence: My mother-in-law works at the bus stop near the swimming pool.
I'll meet you at the bus stop.
I paid by credit card.
She wrote in her notebook.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 作为单个名词单位运作；复数形式通常在主要名词上
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Functions as a single noun unit; plural form usually on the main noun

subTypes.name: 单数名词
subTypes.definition: 指一个人、地点、事物或想法的名词
subTypes.definitionEn: Nouns that refer to one person, place, thing, or idea

examples.sentence: A child is playing in the garden.
The teacher explained the lesson.
A bird is singing outside.
This book is interesting.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 使用单数动词一致；与a/an或one连用
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Takes singular verb agreement; used with 'a/an' or 'one'

subTypes.name: 复数名词
subTypes.definition: 指多于一个人、地点、事物或想法的名词
subTypes.definitionEn: Nouns that refer to more than one person, place, thing, or idea

formationRules.example: man → men, child → children, tooth → teeth, foot → feet, mouse → mice

examples.sentence: The children are playing with their toys.
The cats are sleeping.
Brush your teeth twice a day.
The mice ate the cheese.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 使用复数动词一致
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Takes plural verb agreement

categories.usageNotes: 名词是句子的核心成分之一，几乎所有句子都需要名词来充当主语或宾语。注意可数与不可数的区别，以及专有名词的大小写规则。
categories.usageNotesEn: Nouns are one of the core components of sentences; almost all sentences require nouns to function as subjects or objects. Note the distinction between countable and uncountable nouns, and capitalization rules for proper nouns.

commonErrors.correct: I have a lot of homework.
commonErrors.explanation: homework是不可数名词，不能加-s
honest以元音音素开头，应用an
sheep单复数同形

categories.name: 代词
categories.definition: 代词是用来替代名词或名词短语（先行词）的词。代词有助于避免重复，使句子更简洁连贯。
categories.definitionEn: A pronoun is a word that is used to substitute for a noun or noun phrase (the antecedent). Pronouns help avoid repetition and make sentences more concise and coherent.
categories.functionEn: Substitutes for nouns to avoid repetition

subTypes.name: 人称代词
subTypes.definition: 用来指代特定的人或事物的代词；随人称、数、格和性而变化
subTypes.definitionEn: Pronouns used to refer to specific people or things; vary by person, number, case, and gender

examples.sentence: She gave the book to him.
We are going to the park.
They invited us to dinner.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 主格代词充当句子主语；宾格代词充当动词或介词的宾语
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Subject pronouns function as sentence subjects; object pronouns function as objects of verbs or prepositions

subTypes.name: 物主代词
subTypes.definition: 表示所有或拥有的代词；有两种形式——物主限定词（在名词前）和独立物主代词（独立使用）
subTypes.definitionEn: Pronouns that indicate ownership or possession; have two forms — possessive determiners (before nouns) and independent possessive pronouns (standing alone)

examples.sentence: This is my book. That book is hers.
examples.meaning: 这是我的书。那本书是她的。

examples.sentence: Is this your pen? No, it's his.
examples.meaning: 这是你的笔吗？不，是他的。

examples.sentence: Our house is bigger than theirs.
examples.meaning: 我们的房子比他们的更大。

subTypes.grammarNotes: 物主限定词形式总是在名词前；独立形式独立使用
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Determiner form always precedes a noun; independent form stands alone

subTypes.name: 反身代词
subTypes.definition: 指回句子主语的代词；通过在物主限定词后加'-self'（单数）或'-selves'（复数）构成
subTypes.definitionEn: Pronouns that refer back to the subject of the sentence; formed by adding '-self' (singular) or '-selves' (plural) to the possessive determiner

examples.sentence: She taught herself to play the piano.
I cut myself while cooking.
They enjoyed themselves at the party.
examples.meaning: 他们在派对上玩得很开心。

subTypes.grammarNotes: 当宾语指代与主语同一人/物时充当宾语；也可作为强调代词
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Functions as object when it refers to the same person/thing as the subject; can also function as an intensive/emphatic pronoun

subTypes.name: 强调代词
subTypes.definition: 形式与反身代词相同，但用于强调名词或代词；去掉后不会改变句子的基本含义
subTypes.definitionEn: Same form as reflexive pronouns but used to emphasize a noun or pronoun; can be removed without changing the basic meaning

examples.sentence: The president himself made the announcement.
examples.meaning: 总统本人宣布了这一消息。

examples.sentence: I myself will take care of it.
She wrote the letter herself.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 强调先行词；通常直接放在所强调的名词/代词之后
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Emphasizes the antecedent; usually placed directly after the noun/pronoun it emphasizes

subTypes.name: 指示代词
subTypes.definition: 指出或识别特定事物的代词；表示空间或时间上的近远
subTypes.definitionEn: Pronouns that point to or identify specific things; indicate proximity or distance in space or time

examples.sentence: This is my favorite. I don't like those.
examples.meaning: 这是我最喜欢的。我不喜欢那些。

examples.sentence: These are delicious. Can I have some more?
examples.meaning: 这些很好吃。我能再要一些吗？

examples.sentence: That was a great movie.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 可以充当主语、宾语或补语；替代名词短语
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Can function as subject, object, or complement; replaces a noun phrase

subTypes.name: 疑问代词
subTypes.definition: 用来询问关于人、事物或信息的代词
subTypes.definitionEn: Pronouns used to ask questions about people, things, or information

examples.sentence: Who called you?
What are you doing?
Whose book is this?
Which do you prefer?

subTypes.grammarNotes: 引导疑问句；who用于主语，whom用于宾语（正式），whose用于所有格，which/what用于事物
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Introduces questions; 'who' for subjects, 'whom' for objects (formal), 'whose' for possession, 'which/what' for things

subTypes.name: 关系代词
subTypes.definition: 引导关系（形容词）从句并将其与主句连接起来的代词
subTypes.definitionEn: Pronouns that introduce relative (adjective) clauses and connect them to the main clause

examples.sentence: The woman who called you is my sister.
examples.meaning: 给你打电话的那位女士是我姐姐。

examples.sentence: This is the book that I told you about.
examples.meaning: 这是我跟你说过那本书。

examples.sentence: The man whose car was stolen reported it to the police.
examples.meaning: 车被偷的那位男士向警察报了案。

subTypes.grammarNotes: 引导定语从句，连接从句与主句
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Introduces relative clauses and connects them to the main clause

subTypes.name: 不定代词
subTypes.definition: 指代未指明的人、事物或数量的代词
subTypes.definitionEn: Pronouns that refer to unspecified people, things, or quantities

categories.words: some, somebody, someone, something, anyone, anybody, anything
none, nobody, no one, nothing, neither
many, much, few, little, several, enough, more, most

examples.sentence: Everyone enjoyed the party, but nobody wanted to leave.
examples.meaning: 每个人都喜欢那个派对，但没人想离开。

examples.sentence: Someone left their umbrella behind.
Is there anything I can help with?
examples.meaning: 有什么我可以帮忙的吗？

subTypes.grammarNotes: 可以充当主语、宾语或补语；有些是单数，有些是复数，有些两者皆可
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Functions as subject, object, or complement; some are singular, some plural, some both

subTypes.name: 相互代词
subTypes.definition: 表达两个或多个人或事物之间相互关系的代词
subTypes.definitionEn: Pronouns that express a mutual relationship between two or more people or things

examples.sentence: The two friends love each other.
The team members helped one another.
They haven't seen each other for years.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 充当宾语；表示相互动作或关系
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Functions as object; indicates mutual action or relationship

categories.usageNotes: 代词必须与其先行词在性、数和人称上保持一致。注意区分主格和宾格的用法。
categories.usageNotesEn: Pronouns must agree with their antecedents in gender, number, and person. Distinguish between nominative and objective cases.

commonErrors.error: ~~Me and John went to the store.~~
commonErrors.correct: John and I went to the store.
commonErrors.explanation: 主语应使用主格I，且第一人称应放在后面
介词后应使用宾格me

commonErrors.correct: Everyone should bring his or her book. / Everyone should bring their books.
commonErrors.explanation: 传统语法中everyone视为单数，但现代英语中their作为单数用法已被广泛接受

categories.name: 动词
categories.definition: 动词是表达动作（身体或心理）、事件或存在状态的词。每个完整的英语句子必须包含至少一个动词。动词承载句子的主要信息，并通过时态表达时间。
categories.definitionEn: A verb is a word that expresses an action (physical or mental), occurrence, or state of being. Every complete English sentence must contain at least one verb. Verbs carry the main message of sentences and express time through tense.
categories.function: 表达动作、状态或事件；作为句子的谓语核心
categories.functionEn: Expresses action, state, or occurrence; serves as the core predicate of a sentence

subTypes.name: 实义动词/行为动词
subTypes.definition: 表达身体或心理动作的动词
subTypes.definitionEn: Verbs that express physical or mental actions

subcategories.examples: run, walk, eat, write, kick, jump, read
think, believe, know, remember, understand, consider

examples.sentence: She runs every morning.
He believes in hard work.
They studied all night.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 核心谓语成分；可以是及物或不及物
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Core predicate element; can be transitive or intransitive

subTypes.name: 及物动词
subTypes.definition: 需要直接宾语才能完成意义的动词；动作被传递到宾语
subTypes.definitionEn: Verbs that require a direct object to complete their meaning; the action is transferred to an object
subTypes.examplesList: kick, eat, write, read, buy, make, see, give, tell, love

examples.sentence: He kicked the ball.
She wrote a beautiful letter.
I love chocolate.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 主语 + 及物动词 + 直接宾语 (S + V + O)
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Subject + Transitive Verb + Direct Object (S + V + O)

subTypes.name: 不及物动词
subTypes.definition: 不需要直接宾语就能完成意义的动词；自身就能表达完整的意思
subTypes.definitionEn: Verbs that do not require a direct object to complete their meaning; express a complete thought on their own
subTypes.examplesList: sleep, cry, laugh, arrive, run, happen, exist, fall, rise, die

examples.sentence: The baby slept peacefully.
Birds chirp every morning.
They arrived on time.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 主语 + 不及物动词 (S + V)；可以后接状语
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Subject + Intransitive Verb (S + V); can be followed by adverbials

subTypes.name: 连系动词/系动词
subTypes.definition: 将主语与描述或重命名主语的表语（名词、形容词或短语）连接起来的动词
subTypes.definitionEn: Verbs that connect the subject to a subject complement (noun, adjective, or phrase) that describes or renames the subject
subTypes.primaryVerbs: be (am, is, are, was, were, been, being)
subTypes.otherCommonVerbs: seem, appear, become, feel, look, sound, taste, smell, remain, stay, grow, turn, get, prove

examples.sentence: She is a doctor.
The soup smells delicious.
He became angry.
They seem happy.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 主语 + 连系动词 + 主语补语 (S + V + C)
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Subject + Linking Verb + Subject Complement (S + V + C)

subTypes.name: 助动词
subTypes.definition: 通过添加语法或功能意义（时态、体、语态、语气）来帮助主要动词的动词
subTypes.definitionEn: Verbs that 'help' the main verb by adding grammatical or functional meaning (tense, aspect, voice, mood)

primaryAuxiliaries.usage: 用于进行体和被动语态
用于疑问句、否定句和强调

examples.sentence: She is reading a book.
They have finished their work.
Do you like coffee?
I do understand.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 总是与主要动词一起使用；构成疑问句、否定句、强调陈述和复杂时态
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Always used with a main verb; forms questions, negatives, emphatic statements, and complex tenses

subTypes.name: 情态动词
subTypes.definition: 一种特殊类型的助动词，表达能力、可能性、许可、义务、建议、预测或习惯
subTypes.definitionEn: A special type of auxiliary verb that expresses ability, possibility, permission, obligation, advice, prediction, or habit
subTypes.completeList: can, could, may, might, shall, should, will, would, must, ought to, used to, need (in negatives/questions), dare (in negatives/questions)
keyCharacteristics: 后面总是跟动词原形（不加-s，不加to）
没有不定式或分词形式
不用do来构成疑问句和否定句
keyCharacteristicsEn: Always followed by the base form of a verb (no '-s', no 'to')

modalsTable.primaryMeaning: 过去的能力/可能性/礼貌请求
将来/意图（正式，第一人称）
过去习惯/礼貌请求/条件句

examples.sentence: You must follow the rules.
She can speak three languages.
Would you help me?

subTypes.grammarNotes: 主语 + 情态动词 + 动词原形
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Subject + Modal + Base Verb

subTypes.name: 规则动词
subTypes.definition: 通过在基本形式后加'-ed'构成过去式和过去分词的动词
subTypes.definitionEn: Verbs that form their past tense and past participle by adding '-ed' to the base form

spellingRules.rule: 单音节，CVC模式：双写尾辅音 + -ed
spellingRules.ruleEn: One-syllable, CVC pattern: double final consonant + -ed

examples.sentence: They played football yesterday.
She walked to school.
He studied hard for the exam.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 遵循可预测的变位模式
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Follows predictable conjugation patterns

subTypes.name: 不规则动词
subTypes.definition: 不遵循过去式和过去分词的'-ed'规则的动词；每个都有自己的独特形式
subTypes.definitionEn: Verbs that do not follow the '-ed' rule for past tense and past participle; each has its own unique forms

examples.sentence: She wrote a beautiful poem.
He went to school early.
They have eaten lunch.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 需要逐个记忆不规则形式
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Each irregular verb form must be memorized individually

subTypes.name: 状态动词
subTypes.definition: 描述状态、状况或感情而非动作的动词；通常不用于进行时态
subTypes.definitionEn: Verbs that describe a state, condition, or feeling rather than an action; typically not used in continuous/progressive tenses

categories.verbs: love, hate, like, dislike, prefer, want, need
know, think, believe, understand, remember, forget, agree
be, seem, appear, consist, contain, exist

examples.sentence: I love chocolate.
She knows the answer.
This book belongs to me.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 通常不用于进行时态
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Typically not used in continuous/progressive tenses

subTypes.name: 动态动词
subTypes.definition: 描述动作并可用于进行时态的动词
subTypes.definitionEn: Verbs that describe actions and can be used in continuous/progressive tenses
subTypes.examplesList: run, eat, write, play, jump, think, consider, work

examples.sentence: She is running in the park.
They are playing chess.
He is working on a project.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 可以用于所有进行时态
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Can be used in all continuous/progressive tenses

categories.usageNotes: 动词是英语句子的核心。及物动词需要宾语，不及物动词不需要。情态动词后面总是跟动词原形。状态动词通常不用于进行时态。
categories.usageNotesEn: Verbs are the core of English sentences. Transitive verbs require objects; intransitive verbs do not. Modal verbs are always followed by the base form of a verb. Stative verbs are typically not used in continuous tenses.

commonErrors.explanation: love是状态动词，通常不用于进行时
情态动词后应使用动词原形
home在此处是副词，不需要介词

categories.name: 形容词
categories.definition: 形容词是修饰或描述名词或代词的词。形容词提供有关其所修饰名词的性质、数量或状态的更多信息。它们通常回答'什么样的？''哪一个？''多少？'或'谁的？'等问题。
categories.definitionEn: An adjective is a word that modifies or describes a noun or pronoun. Adjectives provide more information about the qualities, quantities, or states of the nouns they modify. They typically answer questions like 'What kind?', 'Which one?', 'How many?', or 'Whose?'
categories.function: 修饰或描述名词和代词
categories.functionEn: Modifies or describes nouns and pronouns

subTypes.name: 描述性形容词/性质形容词
subTypes.definition: 描述名词的品质、特征或属性的形容词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adjectives that describe the qualities, characteristics, or attributes of a noun
subTypes.examplesList: beautiful, tall, red, strong, delicious, old, young, happy, sad, cold, hot

examples.sentence: She bought a beautiful dress.
examples.meaning: 她买了一条漂亮的裙子。

examples.sentence: The flower is red.
He is a happy boy.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 可用作定语（名词前）或表语（连系动词后）
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Can be used attributively (before noun) or predicatively (after linking verb)

subTypes.name: 数量形容词
subTypes.definition: 表示某物数量或总量的形容词（不指定确切数字）
subTypes.definitionEn: Adjectives that indicate the quantity or amount of something (without specifying the exact number)
subTypes.examplesList: some, much, many, few, a few, little, a little, all, no, enough, several, plenty, more, less, most

examples.sentence: I have some milk.
There are many books on the shelf.
Few people know the truth.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 回答'How much?'或'How many?'；修饰不可数或可数名词
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Answers 'How much?' or 'How many?'; modifies uncountable or countable nouns

subTypes.name: 数词形容词
subTypes.definition: 表示确切数量或顺序的形容词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adjectives that show exact numerical quantity or order

subtypes.examples: one, two, three, ten, hundred, thousand

examples.sentence: There are three students in the room.
She came first in the race.
examples.meaning: 她在比赛中得了第一名。

examples.sentence: I have two brothers.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 基数词表示数量，序数词表示顺序
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Cardinal numbers indicate quantity; ordinal numbers indicate order/position

subTypes.name: 指示形容词
subTypes.definition: 指出或识别特定名词的形容词；表示近远
subTypes.definitionEn: Adjectives that point to or identify specific nouns; indicate proximity or distance
subTypes.examplesList: this (这个), that (那个), these (这些), those (那些)

examples.sentence: This book is interesting.
I like those flowers.
These apples are fresh.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 总是放在所修饰的名词之前；起限定词作用
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Always placed before the noun it modifies; functions as a determiner

subTypes.name: 物主形容词
subTypes.definition: 表示所有、拥有或关系的形容词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adjectives that show ownership, possession, or relationship
subTypes.examplesList: my, your, his, her, its, our, their

examples.sentence: This is my house.
Where is your car?
The dog wagged its tail.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 总是放在名词前；表示某物属于谁
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Always placed before the noun; indicates who something belongs to

subTypes.name: 疑问形容词
subTypes.definition: 用来询问关于名词的问题的形容词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adjectives used to ask questions about nouns
subTypes.examplesList: which (哪一个), what (什么), whose (谁的)

examples.sentence: Which color do you prefer?
Whose book is this?
What time is it?

subTypes.grammarNotes: 放在名词前构成疑问句
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Used before a noun to ask questions

subTypes.name: 分配形容词/个体形容词
subTypes.definition: 指群体中的成员个体而非整体的形容词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adjectives that refer to members of a group individually rather than collectively
subTypes.examplesList: each, every, either, neither, any, both, all

examples.sentence: Each student received a certificate.
examples.meaning: 每个学生都收到了证书。

examples.sentence: Neither answer is correct.
Every child loves candy.

subTypes.grammarNotes: each/every/either/neither接单数名词；both/all接复数名词
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Used with singular nouns (each, every, either, neither) or plural nouns (both, all)

subTypes.name: 专有形容词
subTypes.definition: 由专有名词派生而来的形容词；首字母始终大写
subTypes.definitionEn: Adjectives derived from proper nouns; always capitalized
subTypes.examplesList: French, Italian, American, Chinese, Shakespearean, Victorian, Christian

examples.sentence: I love French cuisine.
We studied Shakespearean drama.
examples.meaning: 我们研究了莎士比亚戏剧。

examples.sentence: She drives an American car.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 修饰名词；表示来源、国籍或关联
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Modifies noun; indicates origin, nationality, or association

subTypes.name: 比较级形容词
subTypes.definition: 用于比较两个事物的形容词；表示较高或较低的程度
subTypes.definitionEn: Adjectives used to compare two things; shows a higher or lower degree

formation.rule: 长形容词（2+音节）more + 形容词

formation.example: good → better, bad → worse, far → farther/further

examples.sentence: She is taller than her sister.
This book is more interesting than that one.
He is a better player than me.
examples.meaning: 他是一个比我更好的球员。

subTypes.grammarNotes: 与than连用比较两者
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Used with 'than' to compare two things

subTypes.name: 最高级形容词
subTypes.definition: 用于比较三个或以上事物的形容词；表示最高或最低的程度
subTypes.definitionEn: Adjectives used to compare three or more things; shows the highest or lowest degree

formation.rule: 长形容词 most + 形容词

formation.example: good → best, bad → worst, far → farthest/furthest

examples.sentence: He is the tallest student in the class.
This is the most beautiful painting.
She is the best singer.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 通常前面加the；比较三个或以上的事物
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Usually preceded by 'the'; compares three or more things

subTypes.name: 分词形容词
subTypes.definition: 由动词的现在分词或过去分词构成的形容词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adjectives formed from present or past participles of verbs

subtypes.examples: boring, interesting, exciting, tiring, amusing, charming
bored, interested, excited, tired, amused, broken

examples.sentence: The movie was boring.
I was bored.
The news was exciting.
She felt excited.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 描述某人/某物如何引起或经历某种感觉；-ing描述引起感觉的事物，-ed描述感受到感觉的人
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Describes how someone/something causes or experiences a feeling

subTypes.name: 复合形容词
subTypes.definition: 由两个或多个词组成的形容词，通常用连字符连接
subTypes.definitionEn: Adjectives made up of two or more words, usually joined by hyphens
subTypes.examplesList: long-term, full-time, high-tech, well-known, good-looking, part-time, blue-eyed

examples.sentence: She has a full-time job.
He is a well-known actor.
This is a long-term project.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 作为单个形容词修饰名词
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Functions as a single adjective modifying a noun

subTypes.name: 限定性形容词
subTypes.definition: 限制或限定名词含义而非描述名词的形容词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adjectives that limit or restrict the meaning of a noun rather than describing it
subTypes.subtypes: 冠词、指示词、物主词、数词、疑问词、分配词
subTypes.subtypesEn: Articles, demonstratives, possessives, numerals, interrogatives, distributives
subTypes.examplesList: the, a, this, my, three, each

examples.sentence: The three old men sat together.
I need some water.
Each person has a ticket.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 识别哪个名词或多少；通常放在描述性形容词之前
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Identifies which noun or how many; typically placed before descriptive adjectives

adjectiveOrder.description: 当多个形容词修饰一个名词时，通常遵循以下顺序
adjectiveOrder.descriptionEn: When multiple adjectives modify a noun, they typically follow this order
adjectiveOrder.example: a lovely little old rectangular brown French wooden coffee table
adjectiveOrder.exampleMeaning: 一张可爱的、小的、旧的、长方形的、棕色的、法国的、木制的咖啡桌

categories.usageNotes: 形容词顺序是学习英语的关键点。描述性形容词通常放在最靠近名词的位置，而限定性形容词放在最前面。比较级和最高级有特殊的构成规则。
categories.usageNotesEn: Adjective order is a key point in learning English. Descriptive adjectives are usually placed closest to the noun, while limiting adjectives come first. Comparative and superlative forms have special formation rules.

commonErrors.explanation: 大小应在颜色之前（顺序：观点→大小→年龄→形状→颜色）
比较级不能用more修饰（除了多音节形容词）
最高级不能用most修饰（除了多音节形容词）

categories.name: 副词
categories.definition: 副词是修饰或描述动词、形容词、另一个副词或整个句子的词。副词通常回答'怎样？''何时？''哪里？''到什么程度？'或'多经常？'等问题。
categories.definitionEn: An adverb is a word that modifies or describes a verb, an adjective, another adverb, or an entire sentence. Adverbs typically answer questions such as 'How?', 'When?', 'Where?', 'To what extent?', or 'How often?'
categories.function: 修饰动词、形容词、其他副词或整个句子
categories.functionEn: Modifies verbs, adjectives, other adverbs, or entire sentences

subTypes.name: 方式副词
subTypes.definition: 描述动作如何执行的副词；通常通过在形容词后加'-ly'构成
subTypes.definitionEn: Adverbs that describe how an action is performed; usually formed by adding '-ly' to adjectives
subTypes.examplesList: quickly, slowly, carefully, badly, well, softly, loudly, beautifully, kindly, patiently

examples.sentence: She sings beautifully.
He drove carefully.
They worked hard.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 回答'How?'；通常放在动词或宾语之后
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Answers 'How?'; usually placed after the verb or object

subTypes.name: 时间副词
subTypes.definition: 指示动作何时发生的副词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adverbs that indicate when an action takes place
subTypes.examplesList: now, then, today, tomorrow, yesterday, soon, later, already, still, yet, immediately, recently, before, after, forever, meanwhile

examples.sentence: I will meet you tomorrow.
Recently, she moved to London.
I have already finished.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 回答'When?'；可以放在句首或句尾
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Answers 'When?'; can be placed at the beginning or end of a sentence

subTypes.name: 地点副词
subTypes.definition: 指示动作在哪里发生的副词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adverbs that indicate where an action takes place
subTypes.examplesList: here, there, everywhere, nowhere, somewhere, anywhere, upstairs, downstairs, inside, outside, abroad, nearby, away, home

examples.sentence: She put her keys there.
Come here, please.
They went abroad for vacation.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 回答'Where?'；通常放在动词或宾语之后
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Answers 'Where?'; usually placed after the verb or object

subTypes.name: 频率副词
subTypes.definition: 指示动作多经常发生的副词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adverbs that indicate how often an action takes place

examples.sentence: I always drink coffee in the morning.
She is usually late.
I never smoke.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 放在主要动词之前或be动词之后
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Placed before the main verb or after the verb 'be'

subTypes.name: 程度副词
subTypes.definition: 描述动作、形容词或另一个副词的程度、强度或级别的副词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adverbs that describe the extent, intensity, or degree of an action, adjective, or another adverb
subTypes.examplesList: very, too, so, quite, rather, fairly, extremely, almost, nearly, just, hardly, barely, completely, totally, absolutely, enough

examples.sentence: She is very happy.
He runs extremely fast.
The test was quite difficult.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 回答'To what extent?'；放在所修饰的形容词/副词之前
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Answers 'To what extent?'; placed before the adjective/adverb it modifies

subTypes.name: 疑问副词
subTypes.definition: 用来询问关于时间、地点、原因、方式或程度的问题的副词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adverbs used to ask questions about time, place, reason, manner, or degree

examples.sentence: When did you arrive?
How did you solve this problem?
examples.meaning: 你是怎么解决这个问题的？

examples.sentence: Why are you crying?
Where do you live?

subTypes.grammarNotes: 引导特殊疑问句
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Introduces questions

subTypes.name: 关系副词
subTypes.definition: 引导关系从句并将其与主句连接起来的副词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adverbs that introduce relative (adjective) clauses and connect them to the main clause

examples.sentence: This is the house where I was born.
Do you know the reason why he left?
examples.meaning: 你知道他离开的原因吗？

examples.sentence: I'll never forget the day when we first met.
examples.meaning: 我永远不会忘记我们第一次见面的那一天。

subTypes.grammarNotes: when指时间，where指地点，why指原因
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: when refers to time, where refers to place, why refers to reason

subTypes.name: 连接副词
subTypes.definition: 像连词一样连接两个独立从句或句子的副词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adverbs that function like conjunctions to connect two independent clauses or sentences
subTypes.examplesList: however, therefore, moreover, furthermore, nevertheless, nonetheless, otherwise, meanwhile, besides, consequently, thus, hence, still, also

examples.sentence: She was tired; however, she continued working.
examples.meaning: 她很累；然而，她继续工作。

examples.sentence: It was raining; therefore, we stayed inside.
examples.meaning: 下雨了；因此，我们待在室内。

examples.sentence: He didn't study; consequently, he failed the test.
examples.meaning: 他没学习；结果，他考试不及格。

subTypes.grammarNotes: 显示从句之间的逻辑关系；通常后面跟逗号或分号
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Shows logical relationship between clauses; usually followed by a comma or semicolon

subTypes.name: 肯定/否定副词
subTypes.definition: 肯定或否定陈述的副词
subTypes.definitionEn: Adverbs that affirm or negate a statement
subTypes.affirmation: yes, certainly, definitely, absolutely, surely, indeed, obviously
subTypes.negation: no, not, never, hardly, scarcely, rarely

examples.sentence: She certainly knows the answer.
He is not coming.
I definitely agree.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 用于肯定或否定整个陈述
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Used to affirm or negate a statement

categories.usageNotes: 副词在句子中的位置比较灵活。方式副词通常放在句尾，频率副词放在动词之前，程度副词放在所修饰的词之前。注意-ly结尾的规律和例外。
categories.usageNotesEn: Adverbs have flexible positions in sentences. Manner adverbs are usually placed at the end, frequency adverbs before the verb, and degree adverbs before the word they modify. Note the -ly pattern and exceptions.

commonErrors.explanation: 修饰动词应用副词carefully，而非形容词careful
fast既是形容词也是副词，没有fastly的形式
频率副词应放在be动词之后

categories.name: 介词
categories.definition: 介词是将名词、代词或名词短语与句子中的另一个词连接起来的词，表示它们之间的关系。介词通常表示时间、地点、方向、方式、原因、目的、施事或拥有的关系。
categories.definitionEn: A preposition is a word that links a noun, pronoun, or noun phrase to another word in the sentence, showing the relationship between them. Prepositions typically indicate relationships of time, place, direction, manner, cause, purpose, agency, or possession.
keyCharacteristics: 介词通常是短词（如in, on, at, to, for, from, of, by, with）
它们总是有宾语（名词或代词）
它们构成介词短语：介词 + 宾语
keyCharacteristicsEn: Prepositions are usually short words (e.g., in, on, at, to, for, from, of, by, with)
They always have an object (a noun or pronoun)
They form prepositional phrases: Preposition + Object
categories.function: 连接词语，表示它们之间的关系（时间、地点、方向等）
categories.functionEn: Links words, showing relationships between them (time, place, direction, etc.)

subTypes.name: 时间介词
subTypes.definition: 指示某事何时发生的介词
subTypes.definitionEn: Prepositions that indicate when something happens

examplesByPreposition.examples: at 5 o'clock, at noon, at night, at Christmas, at the moment
on Monday, on June 1st, on my birthday, on time
in the morning, in July, in 2024, in summer, in the past
during the meeting, during the holiday
for two hours, for a week
since 2020, since last year
before dinner, after class
until tomorrow, till next week

examples.sentence: The meeting starts at 9 AM on Monday.
I have lived here since 2020.

subTypes.grammarNotes: at用于具体时间点，on用于具体日期，in用于较长时段
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: at for specific times, on for specific dates, in for longer periods

subTypes.name: 地点介词
subTypes.definition: 指示某物位于何处的介词
subTypes.definitionEn: Prepositions that indicate where something is located

examplesByPreposition.examples: at the door, at school, at home, at the bus stop
in the room, in London, in the box, in the water
on the table, on the wall, on the floor, on the bus
under the table, under the bed
behind the door, behind the building
between the two buildings
among the crowd
near the station, by the river

examples.sentence: The book is on the table.
She sat beside her friend.
The cat is under the bed.

subTypes.grammarNotes: at用于具体点，in用于封闭空间，on用于表面
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: at for specific points, in for enclosed spaces, on for surfaces

subTypes.name: 方向/移动介词
subTypes.definition: 指示朝向、从某处或穿过某地的移动的介词
subTypes.definitionEn: Prepositions that indicate movement toward, from, or through a location

examplesByPreposition.examples: to school, to the park, to London
into the room, into the water
from home, from the office
through the tunnel, through the park
across the street, across the river
up the hill, down the stairs
past the church, walk past me

examples.sentence: She walked into the room.
They drove through the tunnel.
He jumped over the fence.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 表示运动方向，常与移动动词连用
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Indicates direction of movement; often used with movement verbs

subTypes.name: 方式介词
subTypes.definition: 指示某事如何做的方式的介词
subTypes.definitionEn: Prepositions that indicate how something is done

examplesByPreposition.examples: by hand, by bus, by email, by chance
with a pen, with difficulty, with pleasure
in a hurry, in a loud voice, in silence
like a pro, like me

examples.sentence: She painted the picture by hand.
He wrote the letter with a pen.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 表示做事的方式或手段
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Indicates the manner or means of doing something

subTypes.name: 施事/工具介词
subTypes.definition: 指示谁执行动作（施事）或用什么来执行动作（工具）的介词
subTypes.definitionEn: Prepositions that indicate who performs an action (agent) or what is used to perform it (instrument)

examplesByPreposition.examples: written by Shakespeare, painted by Picasso
cut with a knife, broken with a hammer

examples.sentence: The novel was written by Hemingway.
examples.meaning: 这部小说是海明威写的。

examples.sentence: She opened the door with a key.

subTypes.grammarNotes: by用于被动语态中指施事者，with指工具
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: by for agent in passive voice; with for instrument

subTypes.name: 原因/目的介词
subTypes.definition: 指示某事为何发生或动作的目的的介词
subTypes.definitionEn: Prepositions that indicate why something happens or the purpose of an action

examplesByPreposition.examples: for fun, for a reason, for the sake of
because of the rain, because of you
due to illness, due to bad weather

examples.sentence: He was punished for being late.
The match was canceled because of the rain.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 表示原因或目的
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Indicates cause or purpose

subTypes.name: 拥有介词
subTypes.definition: 指示所有或拥有的介词
subTypes.definitionEn: Prepositions that indicate ownership or possession

examplesByPreposition.examples: the roof of the house, a friend of mine
a man with a hat, the girl with red hair
the key to the door, the answer to the question

examples.sentence: She is a friend of mine.
The key to the door is lost.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 表示所属关系
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Indicates possession or belonging

subTypes.name: 度量介词
subTypes.definition: 指示数量或度量的介词
subTypes.definitionEn: Prepositions that indicate quantity or measurement

examples.sentence: We buy eggs by the dozen.
I want a cup of coffee.
The fabric is sold by the meter.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 表示度量单位
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Indicates units of measurement

commonPrepositions.simple: about, above, across, after, against, along, among, around, at, before, behind, below, beneath, beside, between, beyond, but, by, down, during, except, for, from, in, into, like, near, of, off, on, onto, opposite, out, outside, over, past, since, through, throughout, till, to, toward, under, underneath, until, up, upon, with, within, without
commonPrepositions.compound: inside, into, like, near, onto, opposite, outside, over, past, throughout, toward, underneath, upon, within, without
commonPrepositions.phrasal: according to, ahead of, apart from, as for, because of, due to, except for, instead of, out of, prior to, thanks to, up to, with regard to, in front of, in spite of

categories.usageNotes: 介词是英语学习中的难点之一。关键是记住固定搭配和不同介词表达的不同关系。时间介词at/on/in的区分尤为重要。
categories.usageNotesEn: Prepositions are one of the most challenging aspects of learning English. The key is to memorize fixed collocations and the different relationships expressed by different prepositions. The distinction between time prepositions at/on/in is especially important.

commonErrors.correct: I will meet you on Monday.
commonErrors.explanation: 具体日期前应使用on而非in
married后应使用to而非with
城市等大地点前用in，小地点用at

categories.name: 连词
categories.definition: 连词是连接句子中的其他词、短语或从句的词。连词通过将相关的想法连接在一起，帮助在写作和口语中创造流畅、合乎逻辑的表达。
categories.definitionEn: A conjunction is a word that connects other words, phrases, or clauses within a sentence. Conjunctions help create smooth, logical flow in writing and speaking by linking related ideas together.

subTypes.name: 并列连词
subTypes.definition: 连接同等语法重要性的词、短语或独立从句的连词
subTypes.definitionEn: Conjunctions that connect words, phrases, or independent clauses of equal grammatical importance

fanboys.example: I stayed home, for I was tired.
fanboys.exampleMeaning: 我待在家里，因为我累了。

fanboys.example: He can't swim, nor can he dive.
fanboys.exampleMeaning: 他不会游泳，也不会潜水。

fanboys.example: I wanted to go, but I was sick.
You can have coffee or tea.
It was raining, so we stayed inside.
fanboys.exampleMeaning: 下雨了，所以我们待在室内。

examples.sentence: She wanted to go out, but it was raining, so she stayed home.
examples.meaning: 她想出去，但下雨了，所以她留在了家里。

examples.sentence: I like tea and coffee.
You can stay or go.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 连接语法上同等的成分；连接独立从句时前面用逗号
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Joins grammatically equal elements; when joining independent clauses, preceded by a comma

subTypes.name: 从属连词
subTypes.definition: 将从属（次要从句）与主句连接起来的连词，表示它们之间的特定关系
subTypes.definitionEn: Conjunctions that connect a dependent (subordinate) clause to an independent clause, showing a specific relationship between them

categories.conjunctions: when, while, before, after, until, since, as, as soon as, whenever
categories.example: I will call you when I arrive.
I stayed home because I was sick.

categories.conjunctions: although, though, even though, even if
categories.example: Although it was late, he kept working.

categories.conjunctions: if, unless, provided (that), as long as
categories.example: I will go if you come with me.

categories.conjunctions: so that, in order that, lest
categories.example: She studied hard so that she could pass.
She is taller than her brother.
It was so cold that we stayed inside.

examples.sentence: Although it was raining, we went for a walk.
examples.meaning: 虽然在下雨，我们还是去散步了。

examples.sentence: I will stay here until you come back.
examples.meaning: 我会待在这里直到你回来。

examples.sentence: If it rains, we will cancel the picnic.
examples.meaning: 如果下雨，我们就取消野餐。

subTypes.grammarNotes: 引导从属从句；从属从句不能独立成句
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Introduces dependent clauses; the dependent clause cannot stand alone

subTypes.name: 关联连词
subTypes.definition: 成对使用来连接语法上同等成分的连词
subTypes.definitionEn: Pairs of conjunctions that work together to connect grammatically equal elements

commonPairs.example: Either you leave or I will.
She is not only smart but also kind.
I don't know whether to stay or go.
commonPairs.exampleMeaning: 我不知道是留下还是离开。

commonPairs.example: She is as tall as her mother.

examples.sentence: She is not only intelligent but also hardworking.
Both my brother and sister are doctors.
examples.meaning: 我的哥哥和姐姐都是医生。

examples.sentence: You can either call me or send an email.
examples.meaning: 你可以给我打电话或发邮件。

subTypes.grammarNotes: 连接同等成分；成分必须在结构上平行
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Joins equal elements; the elements must be parallel in structure

subTypes.name: 连接副词
subTypes.definition: 起连词作用但也充当副词的词；它们连接独立从句，同时修饰它们所引导的从句
subTypes.definitionEn: Words that function as conjunctions but also act as adverbs; they connect independent clauses while modifying the clause they introduce
subTypes.examplesList: however, therefore, moreover, furthermore, nevertheless, nonetheless, otherwise, meanwhile, besides, consequently, thus, hence, still, also, accordingly, indeed

examples.sentence: I wanted to go; however, I was too tired.
She studied hard; therefore, she passed.
examples.meaning: 她努力学习；因此，她通过了。

examples.sentence: He is young; moreover, he is inexperienced.
examples.meaning: 他很年轻；而且，他缺乏经验。

subTypes.grammarNotes: 前面需要分号或句号，后面跟逗号；表示逻辑关系
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Requires semicolon or period before it and comma after; shows logical relationships

categories.usageNotes: 连词使句子之间的关系更加清晰。并列连词连接同等成分，从属连词引导从属从句。注意使用关联连词时结构必须平行。
categories.usageNotesEn: Conjunctions make relationships between sentences clearer. Coordinating conjunctions connect equal elements; subordinating conjunctions introduce dependent clauses. Note that correlative conjunctions require parallel structure.

commonErrors.explanation: 连接两个词而非独立从句时不需要逗号

commonErrors.error: ~~Although it was raining, but we went out.~~
commonErrors.correct: Although it was raining, we went out. / It was raining, but we went out.
commonErrors.explanation: although和but不能在同一个句子中使用

commonErrors.correct: Neither he nor I was there.
commonErrors.explanation: neither应与nor搭配，而非or

categories.name: 感叹词
categories.definition: 感叹词是表达突然的情感、感觉或反应的词或短语。感叹词在语法上独立于句子的其余部分——它们在语法上不修饰或连接到其他词。它们常用于非正式的口语和写作中。
categories.definitionEn: An interjection is a word or phrase that expresses a sudden emotion, feeling, or reaction. Interjections are grammatically independent from the rest of the sentence — they do not modify or connect to other words grammatically. They are commonly used in informal speech and writing.
keyCharacteristics: 表达突然的情感（喜悦、惊讶、愤怒、疼痛、厌恶、解脱）
语法上独立——可以单独作为完整的表达
通常用感叹号（强烈情感）或逗号（温和情感）隔开
在口语和非正式语言中更常见
keyCharacteristicsEn: Express sudden emotions (joy, surprise, anger, pain, disgust, relief)
Grammatically independent — can stand alone as a complete utterance
Usually set off by exclamation marks (strong emotion) or commas (mild emotion)
More common in spoken and informal language

subTypes.name: 原发感叹词/单纯感叹词
subTypes.definition: 专门作为感叹词使用的词，不能归类为其他任何词性
subTypes.definitionEn: Words that function exclusively as interjections and cannot be classified as any other part of speech
subTypes.examplesList: Ah, Oh, Ooh, Ow, Ouch, Wow, Phew, Yuck, Eww, Ugh, Huh, Eh, Hmm, Shh, Psst, Boo, Yay, Yippee, Hooray, Hurray, Alas, Whew, Gee, Gosh, Oops, Aha, Ahem, Brr, Pfft, Drat, Duh, Hmph

examples.sentence: Wow! That was incredible!
Ouch! That hurt!
Yay! We won!
Phew! That was close!

subTypes.grammarNotes: 只能作为感叹词使用
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Functions exclusively as interjections

subTypes.name: 继发感叹词
subTypes.definition: 来自其他词类（名词、形容词、动词等）在某些语境中用作感叹词的词
subTypes.definitionEn: Words from other parts of speech (nouns, adjectives, verbs, etc.) that are used as interjections in certain contexts

examplesBySource.examples: goodness, heavens, hell, man, boy, congratulations
great, excellent, nice, awful, terrible, amazing
holy cow, oh my God, dear me, my goodness, oh boy, no way

examples.sentence: Goodness! How did you do that?
Congratulations! You did it!
Great! Let's get started.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 在其他词性基础上作为感叹词使用
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Used as interjections based on their primary word class

subTypes.name: 温和感叹词
subTypes.definition: 表达相对温和情感的感叹词；通常用逗号与句子的其余部分隔开
subTypes.definitionEn: Interjections expressing relatively mild emotions; typically separated from the rest of the sentence by commas
subTypes.examplesList: well, oh, ah, hmm, indeed, yes, no, okay, hey

examples.sentence: Well, I never thought I'd see the day.
examples.meaning: 嗯，我从没想过会看到这一天。

examples.sentence: Oh, I didn't know that.
Hey, how are you doing?

subTypes.grammarNotes: 后跟逗号
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Followed by a comma

subTypes.name: 强烈感叹词
subTypes.definition: 表达强烈情感或爆发的感叹词；通常用感叹号隔开
subTypes.definitionEn: Interjections expressing strong emotions or outbursts; typically separated by exclamation marks
subTypes.examplesList: wow, ouch, yikes, hooray, alas, yippee, bingo, oops, aha

examples.sentence: Yay! I finally passed!
Alas! We lost the game.
Oops! I made a mistake.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 后跟感叹号
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Followed by an exclamation mark

subTypes.name: 情感感叹词
subTypes.definition: 表达情感和感觉的感叹词
subTypes.definitionEn: Interjections that express emotions and feelings

emotionTypes.examples: wow, oh, whoa, gosh, gee, goodness

examples.sentence: Yippee! We won the match!
Alas! I couldn't be there.
Yikes! That was scary!

subTypes.grammarNotes: 按情感类型分类
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Classified by emotion type

subTypes.name: 意志感叹词
subTypes.definition: 表达命令、请求或愿望的感叹词；替代'我想要'的表达
subTypes.definitionEn: Interjections that express commands, requests, or wishes; replace 'I want' expressions
subTypes.examplesList: shh, ahem, psst, hey, please, hush, boo, bravo, encore

examples.sentence: Shh! Be quiet!
Ahem! I need your attention.
Bravo! Well done!

subTypes.grammarNotes: 表达意志或命令
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Expresses will or command

subTypes.name: 认知感叹词
subTypes.definition: 表达想法、理解或领悟的感叹词
subTypes.definitionEn: Interjections that express thoughts, understanding, or realization
subTypes.examplesList: well, aha, oh, I see, hmm, indeed

examples.sentence: Well, I'll try harder.
Aha! Now I understand.
Hmm, let me think about it.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 表达认知或思维过程
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Expresses cognition or thought processes

categories.usageNotes: 感叹词在正式写作中使用较少。标点符号很重要——强烈感叹词用感叹号，温和感叹词用逗号。感叹词可以出现在句首、句中或句尾。
categories.usageNotesEn: Interjections are used less in formal writing. Punctuation is important — strong interjections use exclamation marks, mild ones use commas. Interjections can appear at the beginning, middle, or end of sentences.

commonErrors.correct: 在正式写作中应谨慎使用感叹词和感叹号
commonErrors.explanation: 感叹号过多会使写作显得夸张且不专业

commonErrors.error: 在学术论文中使用感叹词
commonErrors.correct: 避免在正式学术写作中使用感叹词
commonErrors.explanation: 感叹词属于非正式用语

categories.name: 冠词
categories.definition: 冠词是放在名词前的小词，用来指示名词是特指/确定的还是泛指/不确定的。冠词是限定词的一种。在现代语法中，冠词通常被归类为限定词的一个子类，但一些传统语法将其视为独立的词性。
categories.definitionEn: An article is a small word that comes before a noun to indicate whether the noun is specific/definite or general/indefinite. Articles are a type of determiner. In modern grammar, articles are often classified as a subcategory of determiners, but some traditional grammars treat them as a separate part of speech.
categories.function: 表示名词的特指或泛指
categories.functionEn: Indicates definiteness or indefiniteness of nouns

subTypes.name: 定冠词
subTypes.definition: 定冠词'the'指的是说话者和听者都知道的特定名词
subTypes.definitionEn: The article 'the' refers to a specific noun that is known to both the speaker and listener

usageRules.rule: 名词是独一无二或某种类唯一的
usageRules.ruleEn: When the noun is unique or one of a kind
usageRules.examples: the sun, the moon, the earth

usageRules.ruleEn: When the noun has been previously mentioned
usageRules.examples: I saw a cat. The cat was black.

usageRules.ruleEn: When the noun is defined by a following phrase
usageRules.examples: the book on the table, the girl in red
the tallest building, the best student
the first day, the third chapter

usageRules.rule: 与某些专有名词连用（河流、海洋、山脉）
usageRules.ruleEn: With certain proper nouns (rivers, oceans, mountain ranges)
usageRules.examples: the Nile, the Pacific, the Alps

usageRules.rule: 与指代整个群体的形容词连用
usageRules.ruleEn: With adjectives referring to a whole group
usageRules.examples: the rich, the elderly, the poor

examples.sentence: I am reading the book you recommended.
examples.meaning: 我正在读你推荐的那本书。

examples.sentence: The sun rises in the east.
She plays the piano beautifully.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 用于特指人或事物
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Used for specific or particular nouns

subTypes.name: 不定冠词
subTypes.definition: 不定冠词'a'和'an'指的是非特定或一般的名词，首次引入某物
subTypes.definitionEn: The articles 'a' and 'an' refer to a non-specific or general noun, introducing it for the first time

forms.usage: 用于以辅音音素开头的词前
forms.examples: a cat, a university (/juː/), a one-eyed man

forms.usage: 用于以元音音素开头的词前
forms.examples: an apple, an hour (/aʊər/), an honest man

usageRules.rule: 首次提及的单数可数名词
usageRules.ruleEn: For singular countable nouns being mentioned for the first time

usageRules.rule: 表示某类或某职业的一员
usageRules.ruleEn: To indicate membership of a class or profession
usageRules.examples: She is a doctor. He is an engineer.

examples.sentence: I saw an old owl.
examples.meaning: 我看见了一只老猫头鹰。

examples.sentence: She is a talented musician.
examples.meaning: 她是一位有才华的音乐家。

examples.sentence: What a lovely day!

subTypes.grammarNotes: a用于辅音音素前，an用于元音音素前
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: 'a' before consonant sounds, 'an' before vowel sounds

subTypes.name: 零冠词
subTypes.definition: 在可能期望使用冠词的名词前不使用冠词；某些名词不使用冠词
subTypes.definitionEn: The absence of an article before a noun where one might be expected; certain nouns do not take articles

usageRules.ruleEn: With abstract nouns in general sense
With plural countable nouns in general

usageRules.examples: at home, at school, in bed, at work

examples.sentence: I love music.
Honesty is important.
She speaks English.
Children love toys.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 零冠词用于泛称和抽象概念
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Zero article is used for generalizations and abstract concepts

categories.usageNotes: 冠词的选择取决于名词是可数还是不可数，是单数还是复数，是特指还是泛指。这是英语学习者最常遇到的难点之一。
categories.usageNotesEn: Article choice depends on whether the noun is countable or uncountable, singular or plural, specific or general. This is one of the most common difficulties for English learners.

commonErrors.explanation: 单数可数名词前需要冠词
honest以元音音素开头，应用an
泛指音乐时不需要冠词

categories.name: 限定词
categories.definition: 限定词是指定、识别或量化其后的名词或名词短语的词或词组。限定词位于名词短语的开头，提供有关确定性、熟悉度、位置、数量、拥有或数目的信息。现代语法将限定词视为与形容词不同的独立词类。
categories.definitionEn: A determiner is a word or group of words that specifies, identifies, or quantifies the noun or noun phrase that follows it. Determiners come at the start of a noun phrase and provide information about definiteness, familiarity, location, quantity, possession, or number. Modern grammar treats determiners as a separate word class distinct from adjectives.
keyDifferencesFromAdjectives: 没有比较级/最高级形式：限定词没有比较等级（没有'the-er', 'the-est'）
固定位置：限定词总是在名词短语中位于形容词之前
封闭类：限定词构成封闭类（很少添加新词），而形容词是开放类
功能词：限定词是功能词（语法意义），形容词是实词（词汇意义）
不能作主语补语：限定词不能像形容词那样跟在连系动词后面
keyDifferencesFromAdjectivesEn: No comparative/superlative forms: Determiners do not have degrees of comparison
Fixed position: Determiners always come before adjectives in noun phrases
Closed class: Determiners form a closed class, while adjectives are an open class
Function words: Determiners are function words (grammatical meaning), adjectives are content words (lexical meaning)
Cannot be subject complements: Determiners cannot follow linking verbs as adjectives can

subTypes.name: 冠词限定词
subTypes.definition: 最常见的限定词；指示确定或不确定
subTypes.definitionEn: The most common determiners; indicate definiteness or indefiniteness

subTypes.name: 指示限定词
subTypes.definition: 指示名词的空间或时间近远的限定词
subTypes.definitionEn: Determiners that indicate the spatial or temporal proximity of a noun

examples.sentence: This cat is mine.
I don't like those people.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 指代特定人或事物的远近
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Indicates proximity or distance of specific people or things

subTypes.name: 物主限定词
subTypes.definition: 指示所有或拥有的限定词
subTypes.definitionEn: Determiners that indicate ownership or possession

examples.sentence: Where is my coffee?
I like your new car.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 表示所属关系
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Shows possession or ownership

subTypes.name: 数量限定词/量词
subTypes.definition: 指示数量或总量的限定词
subTypes.definitionEn: Determiners that indicate quantity or amount

forms.usage: 肯定陈述中与复数/不可数名词连用
否定句和疑问句中使用

examples.sentence: Are there any questions?
I have several ideas.
There is some water in the glass.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 注意可数与不可数的区别
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Note the distinction between countable and uncountable nouns

subTypes.name: 数词限定词
subTypes.definition: 表达数量或顺序的限定词
subTypes.definitionEn: Determiners that express numerical quantity or order

examples.sentence: I have two brothers.
This is my first visit.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 基数词表示数量，序数词表示顺序
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Cardinal numbers indicate quantity; ordinal numbers indicate order

subTypes.name: 分配限定词
subTypes.definition: 指群体中个体成员的限定词
subTypes.definitionEn: Determiners that refer to individual members of a group

examples.sentence: Every child received a gift.
examples.meaning: 每个孩子都收到了礼物。

examples.sentence: Both options are valid.
Each student must participate.

subTypes.grammarNotes: each/every接单数，both/all接复数
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: each/every with singular, both/all with plural

subTypes.name: 疑问限定词
subTypes.definition: 用来提问的限定词
subTypes.definitionEn: Determiners used to ask questions

examples.sentence: Which color do you prefer?
Whose car is that?
What book are you reading?

subTypes.grammarNotes: 用于构成疑问句
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Used to form questions

subTypes.name: 关系限定词
subTypes.definition: 用来引导关系从句的限定词
subTypes.definitionEn: Determiners used to introduce relative clauses

examples.sentence: Take whichever seat you prefer.
examples.meaning: 你喜欢哪个座位就坐哪个。

examples.sentence: She'll eat whatever you cook.

subTypes.grammarNotes: 引导关系从句
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Introduces relative clauses

subTypes.name: 差异词
subTypes.definition: 指示差异或替代的限定词
subTypes.definitionEn: Determiners that indicate difference or alternative

examples.sentence: I'd like another cup of tea.
Do you have other options?

subTypes.grammarNotes: 表示另外的人或事物
subTypes.grammarNotesEn: Indicates additional or different people or things

determinerOrder.description: 当多个限定词一起出现时，遵循以下顺序：前限定词 → 中限定词 → 后限定词
determinerOrder.descriptionEn: When multiple determiners appear together, they follow this order: Predeterminer → Central Determiner → Postdeterminer
determinerOrder.example: All the many problems / Both my two sisters / Half a dozen eggs

categories.usageNotes: 限定词总是位于名词短语的最前面。注意限定词与形容词的区别——限定词没有比较级和最高级，位置固定。多个限定词同时出现时要遵循限定词顺序。
categories.usageNotesEn: Determiners always come at the beginning of a noun phrase. Note the difference between determiners and adjectives — determiners have no comparative or superlative forms and have fixed positions. Multiple determiners must follow the determiner order.

commonErrors.explanation: 物主限定词和冠词不能同时使用
指示限定词和不定冠词不能同时使用
much用于不可数名词，many用于可数名词

overview.definition: 英语时态系统由12种基本时态组成，通过时间（现在、过去、将来）和体（一般、进行、完成、完成进行）两个维度构成矩阵。每种时态都有特定的结构、用法和时间标志词。
overview.definitionEn: The English tense system consists of 12 basic tenses, forming a matrix through two dimensions: time (present, past, future) and aspect (simple, continuous, perfect, perfect continuous). Each tense has specific structures, usages, and time markers.

timeDimensions.description: 发生在现在或经常性发生的动作

aspectDimensions.description: 在参考时间之前完成的动作
在参考时间之前持续的动作，强调持续时间

affirmative.formula: Subject + V1 (base form); 3rd person singular adds -s/-es

examples.sentence: I work every day.
She works hard.
I don't like coffee.
She doesn't smoke.
Do you speak English?
Does he live here?

usages.explanation: 表示经常发生的行为或习惯

examples.sentence: I drink coffee every morning.
She goes to school every day.

usages.explanation: 表达科学事实或普遍真理

examples.sentence: Water boils at 100°C.
The Earth revolves around the Sun.

usages.explanation: 描述长期存在的状态

examples.sentence: I live in Beijing.
She works as a teacher.

usages.explanation: 用于描述固定时间表

examples.sentence: The train leaves at 8 pm.
My class starts at 9 am every Monday.
examples.meaning: 我的课每周一早9点开始。

usages.explanation: 用于体育解说或新闻标题

examples.sentence: He shoots, he scores!

usages.explanation: 用于给出指示

examples.sentence: You turn left at the corner.

commonErrors.explanation: 第三人称单数应使用doesn't，动词还原为原形

commonErrors.correct: He goes to school every day.
commonErrors.explanation: 第三人称单数动词需加-s/-es

comparisonWith.distinction: 一般现在时表示习惯/永久状态；现在进行时表示此刻正在进行的动作
comparisonWith.exampleThis: I work in an office. (习惯)
comparisonWith.exampleOther: I'm working on a project. (此刻)

examples.sentence: I am reading a book.
She is watching TV.
He isn't working today.
They aren't coming.
Are you listening?
Is it raining?

usages.explanation: 表示说话时正在发生的动作

examples.sentence: I'm writing a letter.
They're playing football.

usages.explanation: 描述临时的状态或情况

examples.sentence: He is staying with friends this week.
I am living in Barcelona for a few months.
examples.meaning: 我在巴塞罗那暂住几个月。

usages.explanation: 表示正在发生的变化

examples.sentence: Electric car ownership is growing rapidly.
examples.meaning: 电动车拥有量正在快速增长。

examples.sentence: It's getting colder.

usages.explanation: 用于表示已经安排好的未来计划

examples.sentence: I'm meeting Jane at 8 o'clock on Saturday.
We're flying to London next week.

usages.explanation: 与always/constantly连用表示不满

examples.sentence: You're always losing your keys!
He's constantly interrupting me!

commonErrors.explanation: 状态动词know不用于进行时
进行时需要用V-ing形式

examples.sentence: I have finished my work.
She has left.
I haven't seen him.
She hasn't eaten yet.
Have you been to Paris?
Has he called?

usages.usage: 未提及具体时间的过去事件
usages.usageEn: Past events when time is not mentioned
usages.explanation: 过去的动作与现在有联系

examples.sentence: I've broken my arm.
She's found a job.

usages.explanation: 一生中曾经有过的经历

examples.sentence: I have been to Paris three times.
Have you ever seen a ghost?

usages.explanation: 表示最近发生的动作

examples.sentence: Tony has just called.
I've already finished my homework.

usages.explanation: 使用for/since表示持续时间

futureInThePast.name: 过去将来时
futureInThePast.definition: 过去将来时是一种复合时态，用于从过去的角度看将要发生的事件。对于尚未发生、仅在过去预期会发生的未来事件，这种时态非常有用。
futureInThePast.definitionEn: Future in the Past is a compound tense used to refer to events that were seen as future from a past perspective. This tense is useful for events that were anticipated in the past but have not yet occurred.

passiveVoice.name: 被动语态
passiveVoice.definition: 被动语态改变句子焦点，将主语（施事者）移至介词by之后或完全省略，将原来的宾语提升为新的主语。被动语态通过将to be的某种形式与主动词的过去分词结合来形成。
passiveVoice.definitionEn: The passive voice changes the focus of the sentence by moving the subject (agent) after 'by' or omitting it entirely, and promoting the original object to the new subject. The passive voice is formed by combining a form of 'to be' with the past participle of the main verb.
passiveVoice.formation: 被动语态 = be动词（对应时态） + 过去分词（V3）
passiveVoice.formationEn: Passive Voice = be verb (appropriate tense) + past participle (V3)

sequenceOfTenses.name: 时态一致/时态呼应
sequenceOfTenses.definition: 时态一致是指在复合句和复杂句中，尤其是含有从句的句子中，时态应遵循逻辑关系。当主句为过去时态时，从句通常也使用相应的过去时态。这一原则确保叙述的时间线保持一致且易于理解。
sequenceOfTenses.definitionEn: Sequence of Tenses refers to the principle that tenses should follow a logical relationship in compound and complex sentences, especially those containing dependent clauses. When the main clause is in a past tense, subordinate clauses typically also use corresponding past tenses.

rules.rule: 主句现在时 → 从句任意时态
rules.ruleEn: Main clause present tense → Subordinate clause any tense

当从句的动作与主句同时发生且仍然有效时

When the action in the subordinate clause is simultaneous with the main clause and still valid

stativeVerbs.name: 状态动词
stativeVerbs.definition: 状态动词（也称为状态动词）描述状态、情感、关系和感官，而非动作。与描述活动的动态动词不同，状态动词通常不用于进行时态，因为它们描述的是静态状况，而不是正在进行的动作。
stativeVerbs.definitionEn: Stative verbs (also called state verbs) describe states, emotions, relationships, and senses rather than actions. Unlike dynamic verbs which describe activities, stative verbs are generally not used in continuous/progressive tenses because they describe static conditions rather than ongoing actions.

exceptions: 当描述有意识的短暂体验时，一些感官动词可用于进行时（I'm tasting the soup.）

用于强调变化或临时状态时（He's being silly.）
exceptionsEn: When describing conscious, temporary experiences, some sensory verbs can be used in continuous form
When the meaning changes, some stative verbs can be used in continuous form
When emphasizing change or temporary state
