overview.definition: 英语句子是由单词按照语法规则组合而成的有意义的表达。理解句子分析的三个基本维度——句子成分、句子类型和句子结构——是掌握英语句法的关键。
overview.definitionEn: An English sentence is a meaningful expression formed by words combined according to grammatical rules. Understanding the three fundamental dimensions of sentence analysis — sentence elements, sentence types, and sentence structures — is key to mastering English syntax.
components: 句子成分 (Sentence Elements): 构成句子的各个部分（主语、谓语、宾语、表语、定语、状语、补语、同位语）
句子类型 (Sentence Types): 按用途（陈述、疑问、祈使、感叹）和结构（简单、并列、复合、复杂）分类
从句 (Clauses): 名词性从句、形容词性从句、副词性从句
structures: 简单句 (Simple Sentence): 只有一个主谓结构
并列句 (Compound Sentence): 两个或多个独立分句用并列连词连接
复合句 (Complex Sentence): 一个独立分句 + 一个或多个从属分句
并列复合句 (Compound-Complex Sentence): 两个独立分句 + 一个或多个从属分句

sentenceElements.name: 主语
sentenceElements.definition: 主语是句子的核心成分之一，是执行动作或被描述的人、事物、概念或实体。在英语语法中，主语通常位于句首，决定谓语动词的形式（主谓一致）。
sentenceElements.definitionEn: The subject is one of the core components of a sentence, referring to the person, thing, concept, or entity that performs an action or is being described. In English grammar, the subject typically appears at the beginning of the sentence and determines the form of the predicate verb (subject-verb agreement).
sentenceElements.positionEn: Usually at the beginning of the sentence

types.example: There is a book on the table.

examples.sentence: The teacher explained the lesson.
examples.subject: The teacher

examples.sentence: Running is good for health.
examples.subject: Running

examples.sentence: What he said surprised everyone.
examples.subject: What he said
examples.meaning: 他说的话让所有人惊讶。

sentenceElements.name: 谓语
sentenceElements.definition: 谓语是句子中说明主语'做什么'或'是什么'的成分。谓语以动词为核心，是句子中不可或缺的部分。每个英语句子必须有且只能有一个谓语。
sentenceElements.definitionEn: The predicate is the element in a sentence that describes what the subject 'does' or 'is'. The predicate centers on the verb and is an indispensable part of the sentence. Every English sentence must have one and only one predicate.

types.description: 由一个实义动词或动词短语构成
由两个或多个动词共享同一主语
types.example: He sang and danced at the party.

examples.sentence: The dog is sleeping.
She has been working hard.
He sings beautifully.

sentenceElements.name: 宾语
sentenceElements.definition: 宾语是句子中承受动作的对象。根据其在句中的位置和作用，可分为直接宾语、间接宾语和介词宾语。
sentenceElements.definitionEn: The object is the element in a sentence that receives the action of the verb. Based on its position and function, it can be divided into direct object, indirect object, and prepositional object.

types.description: 承受动作的直接对象
动作的接收者
位于介词之后
types.example: She is looking at the picture.

types.description: 宾语 + 宾补

examples.sentence: She reads books every day.
He gave his mother a flower.
I am interested in music.

sentenceElements.name: 表语
sentenceElements.definition: 表语（也称为主语补语）是位于连系动词之后、说明主语的身份、特征、状态或类别的成分。表语与主语之间存在等同或描述关系。
sentenceElements.definitionEn: The predicative (also called subject complement) is the element that follows a linking verb and describes or identifies the subject. There is an equivalent or descriptive relationship between the predicative and the subject.

types.example: She is the first to arrive.
The problem is that we don't have time.

examples.sentence: She is a teacher.
He looks tired.
The problem is that we don't have enough money.
examples.predicative: that we don't have enough money
examples.meaning: 问题是我们没有足够的钱。

sentenceElements.name: 定语
sentenceElements.definition: 定语是修饰名词或代词的成分，用于限定或描述名词的范围、性质、特征、数量等。
sentenceElements.definitionEn: The attribute is the element that modifies a noun or pronoun, used to limit or describe the scope, nature, characteristics, or quantity of the noun.

types.example: a way to solve the problem
a sleeping baby / a broken window
types.exampleMeaning: 一个熟睡的婴儿 / 一扇破窗

types.example: the book that I bought yesterday

prepositive.description: 前置定语：位于名词之前

postpositive.description: 后置定语：位于名词之后

examples.sentence: The tall building is a bank.
I have something important to tell you.
examples.meaning: 我有重要的事要告诉你。

examples.sentence: The man standing over there is my uncle.
examples.meaning: 站在那边的那个人是我叔叔。

sentenceElements.name: 状语
sentenceElements.definition: 状语是修饰动词、形容词、副词或整个句子的成分，用于说明动作或状态的时间、地点、原因、方式、程度、目的、条件等。
sentenceElements.definitionEn: The adverbial is the element that modifies a verb, adjective, adverb, or the entire sentence, used to indicate the time, place, cause, manner, degree, purpose, or condition of an action or state.

types.example: He was late because of the rain.
She studies hard to pass the exam.
types.exampleMeaning: 她努力学习是为了通过考试。

types.example: He was so tired that he fell asleep.
If it rains, we will stay home.
types.exampleMeaning: 如果下雨，我们就待在家里。

types.example: Although it was raining, we went out.
types.exampleMeaning: 虽然在下雨，我们还是出去了。
他和他的朋友一起来了。

examples.sentence: He arrived yesterday.
She put the book on the shelf.
Although it was late, he kept working.
examples.meaning: 虽然很晚了，他还是继续工作。

sentenceElements.name: 补语
sentenceElements.definition: 补语是用来补充说明主语或宾语的身份、特征、状态或动作的成分，使句子意义更加完整。
sentenceElements.definitionEn: The complement is the element that supplements and describes the identity, characteristics, state, or action of the subject or object, making the sentence meaning more complete.

types.description: 位于连系动词之后，说明主语
位于宾语之后，补充说明宾语

examples.sentence: They made her happy.
He was elected leader.
She named the cat Fluffy.

sentenceElements.name: 同位语
sentenceElements.definition: 同位语是对其前面的名词或代词进行解释、说明或补充的成分，与被说明的词指同一对象。
sentenceElements.definitionEn: The appositive is the element that explains, clarifies, or supplements the noun or pronoun preceding it, referring to the same object as the word it modifies.

types.description: 不用逗号隔开，是理解句子所必需的
types.example: My friend Tom is a doctor.

types.description: 用逗号隔开，提供额外信息
types.example: Beijing, the capital of China, is a beautiful city.
types.exampleMeaning: 北京，中国的首都，是一座美丽的城市。

examples.sentence: Mr. Smith, our teacher, is very kind.
examples.meaning: 史密斯先生，我们的老师，非常和蔼。

examples.sentence: The fact that he lied surprised me.
examples.meaning: 他撒谎的事实让我吃惊。

examples.sentence: We students should study hard.
examples.meaning: 我们学生应该努力学习。

sentenceTypesByPurpose.name: 陈述句
sentenceTypesByPurpose.definition: 陈述句用于陈述事实、描述情况或表达观点。陈述句的语气可以是肯定或否定。
sentenceTypesByPurpose.definitionEn: Declarative sentences are used to state facts, describe situations, or express opinions. The tone can be affirmative or negative.

examples.sentence: She is a teacher.
I don't like coffee.
The Earth revolves around the Sun.

sentenceTypesByPurpose.name: 疑问句
sentenceTypesByPurpose.definition: 疑问句用于提出问题、询问信息或确认某事。疑问句通常以问号结尾，句末语调上扬。
sentenceTypesByPurpose.definitionEn: Interrogative sentences are used to ask questions, seek information, or confirm something. They typically end with a question mark and have a rising intonation at the end.

subtypes.structure: 助动词 + 主语 + 动词 + ...?

examples.question: Do you like English?
examples.answerPattern: Yes, I do. / No, I don't.

subtypes.structure: 疑问词 + 助动词 + 主语 + 动词 + ...?
一般疑问句 + or + 选择部分?

examples.question: Do you want tea or coffee?

subtypes.description: 不需要回答的疑问句，表达强调

examples.question: Isn't it a beautiful day?
examples.meaning: 今天天气多好啊！（不需要回答）

examples.sentence: Have you finished your homework?
What time is it?
You're coming, aren't you?

sentenceTypesByPurpose.name: 祈使句
sentenceTypesByPurpose.definition: 祈使句用于发出命令、请求、指示、建议或邀请。祈使句通常省略主语（隐含主语为you）。
sentenceTypesByPurpose.definitionEn: Imperative sentences are used to give commands, requests, instructions, advice, or invitations. The subject is usually omitted (implied subject is 'you').
sentenceTypesByPurpose.punctuation: 句号 (.) 或 感叹号 (!)

examples.sentence: Please sit down.
Don't touch that!
Have a seat.
Let's go!

sentenceTypesByPurpose.name: 感叹句
sentenceTypesByPurpose.definition: 感叹句用于表达强烈的情感，如喜悦、惊讶、愤怒或赞赏。感叹句通常以感叹号结尾。
sentenceTypesByPurpose.definitionEn: Exclamatory sentences are used to express strong emotions such as joy, surprise, anger, or admiration. They typically end with an exclamation mark.
sentenceTypesByPurpose.structure: What + (a/an) + 形容词 + 名词! / How + 形容词/副词 + ...!

examples.sentence: What a beautiful day!
How wonderful!
What an interesting book this is!
examples.meaning: 这是一本多么有趣的书啊！

examples.sentence: How fast he runs!

sentenceStructures.name: 简单句
sentenceStructures.definition: 简单句是只包含一个独立的主谓结构的句子。简单句可以很短，也可以包含多个成分，但核心只有一个主语和一个谓语。
sentenceStructures.definitionEn: A simple sentence contains only one independent subject-predicate structure. It can be short or contain multiple elements, but has only one core subject and predicate.

examples.sentence: She sings.
The children played in the garden.
I bought a new book yesterday.

sentenceStructures.name: 并列句
sentenceStructures.definition: 并列句由两个或多个独立分句通过并列连词（for, and, nor, but, or, yet, so）或分号连接而成。每个独立分句都有自己的主语和谓语。
sentenceStructures.definitionEn: A compound sentence consists of two or more independent clauses joined by coordinating conjunctions (for, and, nor, but, or, yet, so) or semicolons. Each independent clause has its own subject and predicate.
sentenceStructures.structure: Independent Clause + 并列连词 + Independent Clause
sentenceStructures.coordinatingConjunctions: FANBOYS: for, and, nor, but, or, yet, so

examples.sentence: She likes tea, but he prefers coffee.
examples.meaning: 她喜欢茶，但他更喜欢咖啡。

examples.sentence: I wanted to go for a walk, but it started raining.
examples.meaning: 我想去散步，但开始下雨了。

examples.sentence: He studied hard, so he passed the exam.
examples.meaning: 他努力学习，所以他通过了考试。

sentenceStructures.name: 复合句
sentenceStructures.definition: 复合句由一个独立分句（主句）和一个或多个从属分句（从句）构成。从句通过从属连词（如because, although, when, if, that等）与主句连接。
sentenceStructures.definitionEn: A complex sentence consists of one independent clause (main clause) and one or more dependent clauses (subordinate clauses). Dependent clauses are connected to the main clause through subordinating conjunctions (such as because, although, when, if, that, etc.).
sentenceStructures.structure: Main Clause + 从属连词 + Subordinate Clause

examples.sentence: I will go to the park if it doesn't rain.
examples.meaning: 如果不下雨，我就去公园。

examples.sentence: Although it was late, he kept working.
examples.meaning: 虽然很晚了，他还是继续工作。

examples.sentence: She called me because she needed help.
examples.meaning: 她给我打电话是因为她需要帮助。

sentenceStructures.name: 并列复合句
sentenceStructures.definition: 并列复合句（也称复杂并列句）包含至少两个独立分句和至少一个从属分句。这种句子结构最为复杂。
sentenceStructures.definitionEn: A compound-complex sentence contains at least two independent clauses and at least one dependent clause. This is the most complex sentence structure.
sentenceStructures.structure: Independent Clause + Independent Clause + Subordinate Clause(s)

examples.sentence: I wanted to go for a walk, but it started raining, so I stayed at home.
examples.meaning: 我想去散步，但开始下雨了，所以我就待在家里了。

examples.sentence: She sings, and he plays the guitar while the children dance.
examples.meaning: 她唱歌，他弹吉他，而孩子们在跳舞。

examples.sentence: I didn't go to the party because I was tired, but my sister went.
examples.meaning: 我没去派对因为我很累，但我姐姐去了。

nounClauses.name: 名词性从句
nounClauses.definition: 名词性从句是在句子中起名词作用的从句，可以在句中充当主语、宾语、表语或同位语。名词性从句通常由that, whether, if, what, who, whom, whose, which, when, where, why, how等引导。
nounClauses.definitionEn: A noun clause is a clause that functions as a noun in a sentence, acting as the subject, object, predicative, or appositive. Noun clauses are typically introduced by that, whether, if, what, who, whom, whose, which, when, where, why, how, etc.

types.introducers: that, what, whether, who, when, where, why, how

examples.sentence: What he said is true.
That he will come is certain.
Whether she will come is still unknown.

types.introducers: that, what, whether, if, who, whom, whose, which, when, where, why, how

examples.sentence: I know that he is right.
She asked me where I lived.
I wonder whether he will come.

types.note: that引导的宾语从句中的that常可省略

types.introducers: that, what, whether, who, when, where, why, how

examples.sentence: The problem is that we don't have enough time.
examples.clause: that we don't have enough time
examples.meaning: 问题是我们没有足够的时间。

examples.sentence: This is what I want.
His suggestion is that we should start early.
examples.meaning: 他的建议是我们应该早点开始。

types.function: 解释说明前面的抽象名词
types.introducers: that, whether, how, what, when, where, why
types.commonNouns: fact, idea, news, hope, belief, doubt, thought, suggestion, problem, question, reason, truth

examples.sentence: The news that she won the prize surprised us.
examples.meaning: 她获奖的消息让我们惊讶。

examples.sentence: I have no idea where he has gone.
The fact that he lied disappointed me.
examples.meaning: 他撒谎的事实让我失望。

adjectiveClauses.name: 形容词性从句/定语从句
adjectiveClauses.definition: 形容词性从句（也称定语从句或关系从句）修饰名词或代词（先行词），由关系代词（who, whom, whose, which, that, as）或关系副词（when, where, why）引导。
adjectiveClauses.definitionEn: An adjective clause (also called relative clause or attributive clause) modifies a noun or pronoun (the antecedent), introduced by relative pronouns (who, whom, whose, which, that, as) or relative adverbs (when, where, why).

types.description: 不用逗号隔开，是理解句子所必需的

examples.sentence: The man who is standing there is my teacher.
examples.meaning: 站在那边的那个男人是我的老师。

examples.sentence: This is the book that I bought yesterday.
examples.meaning: 这是我昨天买的那本书。

examples.sentence: I know the girl whose mother is a doctor.
examples.meaning: 我认识那个女孩，她的妈妈是医生。

types.description: 用逗号隔开，提供额外信息，省略后不影响句意
types.relativePronouns: who, whom, whose, which, when, where (不能用that)

examples.sentence: My brother, who lives in New York, is coming to visit.
examples.meaning: 我哥哥住在纽约，他要来看我们。

examples.sentence: Beijing, which is the capital of China, is a beautiful city.
examples.clause: which is the capital of China
examples.meaning: 北京是中国的首都，是一座美丽的城市。

examples.sentence: He arrived on Monday, when we were all busy.
examples.meaning: 他周一到的，那时我们都很忙。

points: that可用于限制性定语从句，指人或物
which可用于限制性或非限制性定语从句，只能指物
非限制性定语从句只能用which，不能用that

prepositionPlacement.example: The man to whom I spoke was very kind.
prepositionPlacement.meaning: 和我说话的那位男士非常和蔼。

prepositionPlacement.example: The man whom I spoke to was very kind.
prepositionPlacement.meaning: 那位男士非常和蔼，我和他说过话。

adverbialClauses.name: 副词性从句/状语从句
adverbialClauses.definition: 副词性从句（也称状语从句）在句子中起副词的作用，修饰主句的动词、形容词或整个句子。状语从句由从属连词引导，可表示时间、地点、原因、目的、结果、条件、让步、方式、比较等。
adverbialClauses.definitionEn: An adverbial clause functions as an adverb in a sentence, modifying the main clause's verb, adjective, or the entire sentence. Adverbial clauses are introduced by subordinating conjunctions and can express time, place, cause, purpose, result, condition, concession, manner, comparison, etc.

types.conjunctions: when, while, as, before, after, since, until, till, as soon as, the moment, once, whenever
types.tenseNote: 主将从现 —— 主句用将来时，从句用一般现在时表示将来

examples.sentence: When I got home, my mother was cooking.
examples.meaning: 当我到家时，妈妈正在做饭。

examples.sentence: I will call you as soon as I arrive.
Don't leave until I come back.
Put the book where you found it.
examples.meaning: 把书放回你找到它的地方。

examples.sentence: You can sit wherever you like.
examples.meaning: 你喜欢坐哪儿就坐哪儿。

types.conjunctions: because, since, as, now that, seeing that

examples.sentence: I stayed at home because it was raining.
examples.meaning: 我待在家里因为下雨了。

examples.sentence: Since you are busy, I will leave now.
examples.meaning: 既然你很忙，我就先走了。

examples.sentence: As it was getting late, we decided to go home.
examples.meaning: 因为天晚了，我们决定回家。

types.conjunctions: so that, in order that, lest, for fear that

examples.sentence: I got up early so that I could catch the first bus.
examples.meaning: 我早起是为了能赶上第一班公交车。

examples.sentence: He spoke slowly in order that everyone could hear.
examples.meaning: 他说得很慢以便每个人都能听到。

types.note: so + 形容词/副词 + that; such + (a/an) + 形容词 + 名词 + that

examples.sentence: He was so tired that he fell asleep immediately.
examples.meaning: 他太累了，结果立刻睡着了。

examples.sentence: It is such a beautiful day that we should go out.
examples.meaning: 今天天气真好，我们应该出去。

examples.sentence: She spoke so softly that I couldn't hear her.
examples.meaning: 她说话太轻了，我听不见。

types.conjunctions: if, unless, as long as, provided (that), on condition that, suppose/supposing

examples.sentence: If it rains tomorrow, we will stay at home.
examples.meaning: 如果明天下雨，我们就待在家里。

examples.sentence: You can't go out unless you finish your homework.
examples.meaning: 除非你完成作业，否则不能出去。

examples.sentence: I will help you as long as you ask.
examples.meaning: 只要你开口，我就会帮你。

types.conjunctions: although, though, even though, even if, no matter what/who/how, whatever, whoever, however

examples.sentence: Although it was raining, we went for a walk.
examples.meaning: 虽然在下雨，我们还是去散步了。

examples.sentence: Even if it rains, I will go out.
Whatever happens, I will support you.
examples.meaning: 无论发生什么，我都会支持你。

types.conjunctions: as, as if, as though, like

examples.sentence: Do as I told you.
He talks as if he knew everything.
examples.meaning: 他说话好像什么都知道似的。

examples.sentence: She treats me as though I were her own child.
examples.meaning: 她待我就像我是她亲生孩子一样。

types.conjunctions: than, as...as, not so/as...as, the more...the more

examples.sentence: He is taller than I am.
She sings as beautifully as her sister.
examples.meaning: 她唱得和她姐姐一样好听。

examples.sentence: The more you practice, the better you will be.
examples.meaning: 你练习得越多，就会越好。

inversion.name: 倒装
inversion.definition: 倒装是指将句子的正常语序（主语在前，谓语在后）颠倒，使谓语动词或助动词位于主语之前。倒装可分为完全倒装（整个谓语移至主语前）和部分倒装（仅助动词移至主语前）。
inversion.definitionEn: Inversion is the reversal of the normal word order of a sentence (subject before predicate), placing the predicate verb or auxiliary verb before the subject. Inversion can be complete (the entire predicate moves before the subject) or partial (only the auxiliary verb moves before the subject).

types.description: 将整个谓语动词移至主语之前

examples.sentence: Here comes the bus!
There goes the bell.
In the garden stood a tall tree.
examples.meaning: 花园里矗立着一棵大树。

examples.sentence: On the table lies a book.
Then came the war.
Gone are the days when we were young.
examples.meaning: 我们年轻的日子一去不复返了。

examples.sentence: Present at the meeting were the manager and all the staff.
examples.meaning: 出席会议的有经理和所有员工。

types.description: 仅将助动词、情态动词或be动词移至主语之前

situations.negativeWords: never, hardly, scarcely, rarely, seldom, little, not, no sooner...than, hardly...when

examples.sentence: Never have I seen such a beautiful sunset.
examples.meaning: 我从未见过如此美丽的日落。

examples.sentence: Hardly had I arrived when it started raining.
Not only did he finish the work, but he also did it well.
examples.meaning: 他不仅完成了工作，而且做得很好。

examples.sentence: Only in this way can we solve the problem.
examples.meaning: 只有用这种方法我们才能解决问题。

examples.sentence: Only after she left did I realize her importance.
examples.meaning: 只有在她离开后我才意识到她的重要性。

situations.situation: so...that / such...that 中的 so/such 置于句首

examples.sentence: So fast did he run that I couldn't catch up.
examples.meaning: 他跑得如此快以至于我追不上。

examples.sentence: Such was his anger that he couldn't speak.
examples.meaning: 他如此愤怒以至于说不出话来。

examples.sentence: Had I known the truth, I would have told you.
examples.meaning: 如果我知道真相，我就会告诉你了。

examples.sentence: Were I you, I would accept the offer.
examples.meaning: 如果我是你，我会接受这个提议。

examples.sentence: Hard as he tried, he couldn't pass the exam.
examples.meaning: 尽管他很努力，他还是没能通过考试。

examples.sentence: I like coffee, and so does she.
I don't like spinach, and neither does he.
examples.meaning: 我不喜欢菠菜，他也不喜欢。

emphasis.name: 强调
emphasis.definition: 强调是指通过特定的语法手段突出句子中的某个成分，使其更加引人注目。英语中常用的强调方式包括：It is/was...that...强调句型、助动词do强调、what从句强调、倒装等。
emphasis.definitionEn: Emphasis refers to highlighting a particular element in a sentence through specific grammatical means to make it more noticeable. Common emphasis methods in English include: cleft sentences with It is/was...that..., auxiliary do, what-clauses, and inversion.

methods.description: 可以强调除谓语以外的任何句子成分（主语、宾语、状语）

examples.original: I met John in the park yesterday.
examples.emphasized: It was I who/that met John in the park yesterday. (强调主语)
examples.meaning: 昨天在公园见到约翰的是我。

examples.emphasized: It was John that I met in the park yesterday. (强调宾语)
examples.meaning: 我昨天在公园见到的是约翰。

examples.emphasized: It was in the park that I met John yesterday. (强调地点状语)
examples.meaning: 我昨天见到约翰的地方是在公园。

examples.emphasized: It was yesterday that I met John in the park. (强调时间状语)
examples.meaning: 我是在昨天在公园见到约翰的。

methods.usageNote: 判断强调句型的方法：去掉It is/was...that...后，剩余部分仍能构成完整句子。

methods.description: 在谓语动词前加do/does/did，表示'确实、真的'
用what引导的名词性从句来强调谓语部分
methods.structure: What + 从句 + is/was + 被强调部分

examples.emphasized: What I need is your help.
What he bought was a book.

examples.sentence: Never have I been so happy!
Only then did I understand the truth.
examples.meaning: 只有到那时我才明白真相。

examples.sentence: This is the very book I was looking for.
examples.meaning: 这正是我一直在找的书。

examples.sentence: At that very moment, the phone rang.

subjunctiveMood.name: 虚拟语气
subjunctiveMood.definition: 虚拟语气用于表示与事实相反或不太可能发生的情况、愿望、建议、命令、必要性等。虚拟语气通过特定的动词形式来表达假设或非真实的含义。
subjunctiveMood.definitionEn: The subjunctive mood is used to express situations contrary to fact or unlikely to happen, wishes, suggestions, commands, and necessity. The subjunctive mood uses specific verb forms to express hypothetical or unreal meanings.

types.type: 与现在事实相反的虚拟
types.ifClause: If + 主语 + 动词过去式 (be动词用were)
types.mainClause: 主语 + would/could/should/might + 动词原形

examples.sentence: If I were you, I would take that job.
examples.meaning: 如果我是你，我会接受那份工作。（我不是你）

examples.sentence: If I had enough money, I would buy a new car.
examples.meaning: 如果我有足够的钱，我会买一辆新车。（我没有足够的钱）

examples.sentence: If she knew the truth, she would tell us.
examples.meaning: 如果她知道真相，她会告诉我们。（她不知道真相）

types.type: 与过去事实相反的虚拟
types.ifClause: If + 主语 + had + 过去分词
types.mainClause: 主语 + would/could/should/might + have + 过去分词

examples.sentence: If I had studied harder, I would have passed the exam.
examples.meaning: 如果我更努力学习，我就会通过考试了。（事实上我没通过）

examples.sentence: If she had known about the party, she would have come.
examples.meaning: 如果她知道派对的事，她就会来了。（她不知道）

examples.sentence: If they had left earlier, they wouldn't have missed the train.
examples.meaning: 如果他们早点出发，就不会错过火车了。

types.type: 与将来事实相反的虚拟
types.ifClause: If + 主语 + should + 动词原形 / were to + 动词原形 / 动词过去式
types.mainClause: 主语 + would/could/should/might + 动词原形

examples.sentence: If it should rain tomorrow, we would cancel the picnic.
examples.meaning: 万一明天下雨，我们就取消野餐。

examples.sentence: If he were to come, what would you say?
examples.meaning: 万一他来了，你会说什么？

types.description: 表达无法实现的愿望

forms.form: wish + 主语 + 动词过去式 (be用were)
wish + 主语 + had + 过去分词
forms.example: I wish I had studied harder.
forms.meaning: 我希望我当时更努力学习。

forms.form: wish + 主语 + would/could + 动词原形
forms.example: I wish he would stop talking.

examples.sentence: If only I had known the truth!
examples.meaning: 要是我知道真相就好了！

examples.sentence: I wish I were a bird.

types.type: 表示建议、要求、命令的虚拟
types.description: 在表示建议、要求、命令等动词后的宾语从句中使用(should) + 动词原形
types.triggerVerbs: suggest, advise, recommend, propose, demand, require, request, order, insist, ask, urge

examples.sentence: I suggest that he (should) study harder.
The doctor recommended that she (should) take a rest.
It is important that he (should) be present.

types.type: as if / as though 虚拟
types.description: 表示与事实不符的比喻

forms.example: He talks as if he knew everything.
forms.meaning: 他说话好像什么都知道似的。

forms.example: He looks as if he had seen a ghost.
forms.meaning: 他看起来好像见了鬼似的。

forms.form: would rather + 主语 + 动词过去式
forms.example: I would rather you stayed here.

forms.form: would rather + 主语 + had + 过去分词
forms.example: I would rather you had told me earlier.

types.description: 表示早该做某事了
types.form: It is (high/about) time + 主语 + 动词过去式

examples.sentence: It is time we went home.
It's high time you made a decision.

types.type: lest / for fear that 虚拟
types.form: lest / for fear that + 主语 + (should) + 动词原形

examples.sentence: He took an umbrella lest it (should) rain.
She spoke softly for fear that she (should) wake the baby.
examples.meaning: 她轻声说话以免吵醒婴儿。

mixedConditionals.description: 混合条件句：条件从句和主句指不同的时间
mixedConditionals.descriptionEn: Mixed conditionals: if-clause and main clause refer to different times

examples.sentence: If I had studied harder (过去), I would have a better job now (现在).
examples.meaning: 如果我当初更努力学习，我现在就会有一份更好的工作。

examples.sentence: If I were rich (现在), I would have bought that house (过去).
examples.meaning: 如果我有钱，我早就买了那栋房子。

ellipsis.name: 省略
ellipsis.definition: 省略是指在句子中省略某些已知或可推断的成分，以避免重复，使表达更简洁。省略必须保证句子的意思仍然清楚完整。
ellipsis.definitionEn: Ellipsis refers to omitting certain known or inferable elements in a sentence to avoid repetition and make expression more concise. Omission must ensure that the sentence meaning remains clear and complete.

types.situations: 祈使句中省略主语you; 口语中省略I
在比较句和并列句中省略重复的谓语

examples.original: He sings better than I sing.

types.situations: 在上下文清楚的情况下省略宾语

examples.original: —Have you finished your homework? —Yes, I have finished it.
examples.meaning: —你完成作业了吗？—是的，完成了。

types.situations: 省略整个从句，尤其是宾语从句

examples.original: —Are you coming? —I hope I am coming.

types.description: 在并列句中省略重复的成分

examples.original: She sings beautifully and she dances gracefully.
examples.ellipted: She sings beautifully and dances gracefully.
examples.meaning: 她唱得美，舞也跳得优雅。

examples.original: Tom is a doctor and Mary is a doctor too.
examples.ellipted: Tom is a doctor and Mary too.

types.description: 在复合句中省略重复的成分

examples.original: Although he is young, he knows a lot.
examples.ellipted: Although young, he knows a lot.

examples.original: When he was asked, he didn't answer.
examples.meaning: 当被问到时，他没有回答。

types.description: 省略不定式中重复的部分，只保留to

examples.original: —Would you like to come? —Yes, I'd love to come.
You can go if you want to go.
examples.ellipted: You can go if you want to.

types.situations: 口语中省略助动词或主语
